session_summary: Planning session. Reviewed remaining tasks (007, 008, 009). Beckham confirmed TASK-007 (user testing) is done — captured user feedback. Refined TASK-008 and TASK-009 scope based on that feedback. Ran a risk analysis subagent. No code written — this was pure design/planning.

workflow_position.step_reached: TASK-008 is next. Plan has been discussed but NOT yet formally approved at the PLAN gate. Next agent should present the plan for Beckham's approval before writing any code.
workflow_position.next_action: Pick up TASK-008. Present the refined plan (see task file) for Beckham's approval. Follow the full task lifecycle: PICK > PLAN (gate) > MODE > EXECUTE > CODE REVIEW (gate) > PR (gate) > CLOSE.

git_state.branching_strategy: per-task — each task gets its own branch off main, merges back via PR

task_status.TASK-007: done — user testing complete, feedback captured in task file outcome field
task_status.TASK-008: planned — NEXT UP. Structured output + prompt tuning. Scope refined with user feedback and risk analysis.
task_status.TASK-009: planned — blocked by TASK-008. UI transformation. Scope refined with user feedback.

positive: Asked useful concrete questions: 'who is your audience?' and 'give me a concrete example'
Great at focusing on the important things
negative: Feels like an interrogation, not collaborative — too many questions, repetitive
Too insistent on problem/solution framing — not every presentation fits that
No markdown formatting — walls of plain text
Takes too long to develop a presentation — needs to be punchier
requested_features: Quick-reply suggestion buttons instead of typing
user_feedback_summary.beckham_synthesis: The goal is creating an experience of the chatbot helping the user 'unpack and build their presentation' — not an interrogation. Structured UI (stepper, cards, slide preview) from TASK-008/009 directly serves this by showing the presentation taking shape. Suggestion buttons turn 'answer my question' into 'pick a direction'. These two worlds (user feedback fixes + original TASK-008/009 vision) are combined, not one replacing the other.

key_technical_decisions.dual_mode_streaming: Text streams progressively via 'text' events. Tool_use metadata (phase, suggestions, slides) accumulates silently via 'inputJson' events. Metadata sent to client after stream completes. This is the critical architectural decision for TASK-008.
key_technical_decisions.full_deck_per_message: Each assistant message in structure/refine carries the complete slides[] array. No incremental diffs. Simpler, avoids state reconstruction bugs, costs more tokens.
key_technical_decisions.forward_only_stepper: Phase can advance but never regress. Protects against LLM outputting inconsistent phase values.
key_technical_decisions.prompt_tuning_in_008: System prompt rewrite folded into TASK-008 rather than a separate task. Collaborative tone, less repetitive, loosen rigid problem framing, add tool-use instructions.

high: Streaming + tool_use: stream.on('text') is blind to tool_use blocks. Must listen for both 'text' and 'inputJson' events. Model must produce text first, then tool call.
medium: max_tokens truncation: structured JSON + text can exceed 2048 tokens in structure/refine phases. Bump to 4096 or dynamic.
LLM phase inconsistency: model might output wrong phase. Mitigate with enum constraint in tool schema + forward-only stepper.
low: Tool definition token cost (~200-400 tokens, but cacheable with prompt caching).
Old conversations with null metadata — straightforward fallback to plain text.

src/server/schema.ts — DB schema, needs metadata column added
src/client/App.tsx — message rendering loop (lines 310-336), streaming state, input handling
src/client/services/requests.ts — streamMsg() SSE parser, needs metadata event handling
